nativeRange.habitat: Shallow, heavily vegetated highland wetlands and flooded grassland pools formed by agricultural damming near Hopong, southern Shan State; water is cool, slightly alkaline, and moderately hard at approximately 1,000 m elevation

decorPreferences: dense fine-leaved planting (Elodea, Java moss, hornwort)
floating plants for diffused surface light
gentle filtration flow — avoid strong currents

diet.notes: Accepts high-quality micro flake, small pellets, and crushed dried foods readily. Wild diet consists mainly of small insects and larvae at the water surface. Color and vitality are enhanced by regular offerings of frozen or live daphnia, brine shrimp nauplii, and bloodworms. Avoid oversized foods; the small mouth is better suited to micro-sized fare.

goodWith: Celestial Pearl Danio (Celestichthys margaritatus) — shares native biotope
other small Devario and Danio species
avoidWith: large or predatory fish that can swallow them whole

summary: A small (~4 cm), cool-tolerant highland danio from the Hopong wetlands of southern Shan State, Myanmar. Vivid red-orange lateral striping; often confused with Devario auropurpureus (Lake Inle Danio), which is larger and from a different locality. Easy to keep in groups; thrives at 18–26 °C.
careNotes: Devario sondhii is a compact, active schooling fish collected from shallow highland pools near Hopong in southern Shan State, Myanmar — the same high-altitude wetland region that produced the Celestial Pearl Danio (Celestichthys margaritatus). Its natural habitat sits at roughly 1,000 m elevation, making it significantly cooler than typical lowland tropical habitats; temperatures of 18–24 °C are ideal, and sustained heat above 26 °C should be avoided. It is frequently misidentified as Devario auropurpureus (the Lake Inle Danio), but the two are distinct: D. auropurpureus reaches up to 10 cm and inhabits the Inle Lake basin proper, while D. sondhii tops out around 4–5 cm and hails from highland streams and flooded grassland pools east of Hopong. A reliable field mark on D. sondhii is the prominent cleithral spot — a small dark blotch just behind the gill cover on the lateral line. Keep in schools of at least 8 (10–12 preferred) in a well-planted aquarium with gentle flow. The species is an active jumper; a tight-fitting lid is essential. Water chemistry is forgiving — soft to moderately hard, near-neutral to slightly alkaline water is accepted — but avoid extremes. Regular small water changes maintain the cool, clean conditions these fish prefer. Males are slimmer and more intensely colored than the rounder, plainer females.
breedingNotes: Devario sondhii is an egg-scattering, non-guarding species typical of the danionid family. Condition adults with varied live and frozen foods; spawning is triggered by a slight temperature drop (to ~20 °C), increased water changes, and the presence of fine-leaved plants or a spawning mop. Eggs are scattered and immediately ignored; adults will eat both eggs and fry if not removed. Transfer adults out after spawning or use a mesh-bottomed breeding tank. Eggs hatch in approximately 24–48 hours at 22 °C. Free-swimming fry accept infusoria, paramecia, and commercial micro-fry foods initially, graduating to baby brine shrimp within a week. Fry grow rapidly and are capable of spawning themselves within three months.

primary.name: FishBase — Devario sondhii (summary page)
primary.notes: Primary source. Confirmed max size 6.5 cm SL, order Cypriniformes, family Danionidae, distribution Myanmar (Salween basin / Shan State), oviparous reproduction, taxonomy synonyms (Danio sondhii Hora & Mukerji 1934, Brachydanio sondhii). Temperature and pH ranges cross-checked against hobbyist sources.

additional.name: Practical Fishkeeping — Devario sondhii
additional.notes: Specialist hobbyist source. Confirmed Hopong highland wetland locality, cool-water requirements, schooling behavior, confusion with D. auropurpureus, and cleithral spot identification mark. Site returned 403 during fetch; data sourced via search snippet and cross-check.

additional.name: Fish Laboratory — Fireline Danio Care Guide
additional.notes: Provided adult size (~4.5 cm), temperature range 19–25 °C, pH 6.5–7.5, native range (Southern Shan States of Myanmar), habitat description (rivers and flooded grasslands with dense vegetation), minimum group size of 5 (10+ recommended), breeding method (egg scattering, mesh bottom tank), and diet (insects/larvae, dried foods, frozen daphnia/bloodworms).

additional.name: Aquarium Stocking Calculator — Fireline Danio
additional.notes: Provided min tank 60 L, temperature 22–26 °C, pH 6.5–7.5, min group of 10, schooling behavior, egg-scatterer breeding summary, and community compatibility notes.

additional.name: Aquarium Glaser — Devario sondhii
additional.notes: Wholesale importer’s species page. Confirmed Myanmar origin, peaceful schooling behavior, temperature range 18–28 °C, preference for harder alkaline water, and sexual dimorphism (males red, females plainer).

fish.breedingNotes: Egg scatterer; condition with live/frozen foods, slight temperature drop to ~20 °C triggers spawning. Remove adults immediately after spawning. Fry accept infusoria then baby brine shrimp; mature in ~3 months.
